---
title: "Accidentally put a launcher up in the &quot;Application/Place&quot; tool bar"
date: 2013-07-13
forum: General Help
---

### Post by RedRat on 2013-07-13
Running 12.04 but using the Gnome interface. I accidentally dragged the Opera application launcher up to the main tool bar at the top of the window and now cannot remove it. I don't really want it there. Right clicking on it does not give me the option of removing it, e.g., deleting it. Any ideas?

---

### Post by gifford on 2013-07-13
Use main menu for gnome. It is available in software center and synaptic. Is this what you meant?

---

### Post by RedRat on 2013-07-13
> **gifford said:**
> Use main menu for gnome. It is available in software center and synaptic. Is this what you meant?

Here is a photo of what I mean. I may not have been clear. As you can see I use the "old-fashioned" Gnome interface and not Unity. I want to remove the Opera launcher from that title bar--I think that is what it is called.

[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]

---

### Post by RedRat on 2013-07-13
Hope this image comes through. Sorry.

[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/;base64,[/IMG]

---

### Post by RedRat on 2013-07-13
OK, I give up. How do I post a small image here to demonstrate what I am talking about? Used to be able to just drag and drop and the jpg comes through. Help!

---

### Post by Mr.Dunham on 2013-07-13
Press the "Alt" key and then your mouse's right button over the icon and you'll see the "Remove from panel" option.

---

### Post by RedRat on 2013-07-13
> **Mr.Dunham said:**
> Press the "Alt" key and then your mouse's right button over the icon and you'll see the "Remove from panel" option.

Sorry this doesn't work. The icon is in the Title Bar of the Gnome main desktop window. I actually have a small jpg screenshot of it but can't seem to post images here. Anyone have an idea of how to do this.

---

### Post by RedRat on 2013-07-13
[ATTACH=CONFIG]244710[/ATTACH]

This is what I mean about the title bar. It is in the upperleft corner.

---

### Post by stinkeye on 2013-07-13
...may also be alt+super+Right-mouse if using compiz in the gnome-classic session.

---

### Post by RedRat on 2013-07-13
> **stinkeye said:**
> ...may also be alt+super+Right-mouse if using compiz in the gnome-classic session.

That did it! Thanks much. Solved.

---

### Post by Mr.Dunham on 2013-07-14
Well, the Alt + right click works most of the cases; when that same problem happened to me, that's the solution I found by looking around here in the forums. Anyways, bottom line is that the fix is easy, you just had to ask ;)

I guess that's because I'm not using Compiz or any effects on my Gnome session. I'll keep in mind **stinkeye**'s solution next time.

---

### Post by stinkeye on 2013-07-14
> **Mr.Dunham said:**
> Well, the Alt + right click works most of the cases; when that same problem happened to me, that's the solution I found by looking around here in the forums. Anyways, bottom line is that the fix is easy, you just had to ask ;)

I guess that's because I'm not using Compiz or any effects on my Gnome session. I'll keep in mind **stinkeye**'s solution next time.
alt+super+Right-mouse works with either gnome-classic session while alt+Right-mouse only works in the no-effects session.
So to save the trouble of posting the 2 different shortcuts I just give alt+super+Right-mouse no matter which session. :)

---
